ns0:CodeList.ns0:Title: Recommendation 20, including Recommendation 21 codes - prefixed with X (UN/ECE)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: group
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of groups (group: set of items classified together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: outfit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of outfits (outfit: a complete set of equipment / materials / objects used for a specific purpose).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ration
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of rations (ration: a single portion of provisions).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of liquid measure, especially related to spirits.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: stick, military
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of military sticks (military stick: bombs or paratroops released in rapid succession from an aircraft).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: twenty foot container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of shipping containers that measure 20 foot in length.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: forty foot container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of shipping containers that measure 40 foot in length.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: theoretical pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the expected mass of material expressed as the number of pounds.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: theoretical ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the expected mass of material, expressed as the number of tons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: sitas
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of area for tin plate equal to a surface area of 100 square metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mesh
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of strands per inch as a measure of the fineness of a woven product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: net kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the total number of kilograms after deductions.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: part per million
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 10⁻⁶.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent weight
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 10⁻².

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: part per billion (US)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 10⁻⁹.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: fixed rate
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of quantity expressed as a predetermined or set rate for usage of a facility or service.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: radian per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO/TC12 SI Guide

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: radian per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO/TC12 SI Guide

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: volt AC
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electric potential in relation to alternating current (AC).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: volt DC
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electric potential in relation to direct current (DC).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per hour
kilobyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10³ (1000) bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: manmonth
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of months for a person or persons to perform an undertaking.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megabyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁶ (1000000) bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megabecquerel
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce la milionesima parte del becquerel

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: batch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of batches (batch: quantity of material produced in one operation or number of animals or persons coming at once).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: MMSCF/day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one million (1000000) cubic feet of gas per day.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hydraulic horse power
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power defining the hydraulic horse power delivered by a fluid pump depending on the viscosity of the fluid.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ampere square metre per joule second
British thermal unit (international table) per second square foot degree Rankine
British thermal unit (international table) per pound degree Rankine
British thermal unit (international table) per second foot degree Rankine
British thermal unit (international table) per hour square foot degree Rankine
deadweight tonnage
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the difference between the weight of a ship when completely empty and its weight when completely loaded, expressed as the number of tons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: decitex
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of yarn density. One decitex equals a mass of 1 gram per 10 kilometres of length.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Rankine
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO 80000-5 (Quantities and units — Part 5: Thermodynamics)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: denier
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of yarn density. One denier equals a mass of 1 gram per 9 kilometres of length.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: 8-part cloud cover
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of eighth-parts as a measure of the celestial dome cloud coverage. Synonym: OKTA , OCTA

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ampere per square metre kelvin squared
freight ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information typically used for billing purposes, defined as either the number of metric tons or the number of cubic metres, whichever is the larger.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: rate
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of quantity expressed as a rate for usage of a facility or service.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: grade

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to one binary digit.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ball
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of balls (ball: object formed in the shape of sphere).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bulk pack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of items per bulk pack.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: activity
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of activities (activity: a unit of work or action).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: byte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 8 bits.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: additional minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of minutes in addition to the referenced minutes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: average minute per call
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of minutes for the average interval of a call.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: access line
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of telephone access lines.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ampere hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electric charge defining the amount of charge accumulated by a steady flow of one ampere for one hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: year
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of time equal to 365,25 days. Synonym: Julian year
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del tempo pari a 365,25 giorni. Sinonimo: anno giuliano

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: anti-hemophilic factor (AHF) unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure for blood potency (US).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: assortment
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of assortments (assortment: set of items grouped in a mixed collection).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: alcoholic strength by mass
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the alcoholic strength of a liquid.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: alcoholic strength by volume
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the alcoholic strength of a liquid (e.g. spirit, wine, beer, etc), often at a specific temperature.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: american wire gauge
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance used for measuring the diameter of small tubes or wires such as the outer diameter of hypotermic or suture needles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: assembly
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of assemblies (assembly: items that consist of component parts).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per pound
bit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to one binary digit per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: joule per metre squared

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per metre to the fourth power
credit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of entries made to the credit side of an account.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: digit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information defining the quantity of numerals used to form a number.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: batting pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of pounds of wadded fibre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gibibit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2³⁰ bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: barrel, imperial
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume used to measure beer. One beer barrel equals 36 imperial gallons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: light year
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of length defining the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gigabit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁹ bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cycle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of cycles (cycle: a recurrent period of definite duration).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gigabit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁹ bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch per linear foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of length defining the number of inches per linear foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: base box
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of area of 112 sheets of tin mil products (tin plate, tin free steel or black plate) 14 by 20 inches, or 31,360 square inches.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: board foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the number of cords (cord: a stack of firewood of 128 cubic feet).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: billion (EUR)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: trillion (US)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred board foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one hundred board foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: beats per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: The number of beats per minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: becquerel
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce l'attività di un radionuclide che ha un decadimento al secondo. Nel sistema internazionale ha sostituito il curie.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: call
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of calls (call: communication session or visitation).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kibibit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2¹⁰ (1024) bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilobit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10³ (1000) bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton metre squared per kilogram squared
octave
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit used in music to describe the ratio in frequency between notes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: one
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: unit
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Definisce l'unità singola.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: phon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of subjective sound loudness. A sound has loudness p phons if it seems to the listener to be equal in loudness to the sound of a pure tone of frequency 1 kilohertz and strength p decibels.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilobit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10³ (1000) bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilovolt ampere hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of accumulated energy of 1000 volt amperes over a period of one hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal cubic metre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: reciprocal second per cubic metre

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal electron volt per cubic metre
coil group
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of coil groups (coil group: groups of items arranged by lengths of those items placed in a joined sequence of concentric circles).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal kelvin or kelvin to the power minus one
reciprocal square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: reciprocal metre squared

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal pascal or pascal to the power minus one
carrying capacity in metric ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the carrying capacity, expressed as the number of metric tons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Celsius
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO 80000-5 (Quantities and units — Part 5: Thermodynamics)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units in multiples of 100.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: card
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units of card (card: thick stiff paper or cardboard).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred leave
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of leaves, expressed in units of one hundred leaves.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della superficie, pari all'area racchiusa da un quadrato avente lati lunghi un centimetro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità volumetrica, equivalente al volume di un cubo con lato di 1 centimetro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura di lunghezza, pari alla centesima parte del metro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred pack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of hundred-packs (hundred-pack: set of one hundred items packaged together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cental (UK)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one hundred weight (US).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: content gram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of grams of a named item in a product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: content ton (metric)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of metric tons of a named item in a product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: curie
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce l'attività di un radionuclide di un grammo dell'isotopo radio-226.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt hour per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of accumulated energy of a thousand watts over a period of one hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lot [unit of weight]
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of weight equal to about 1/2 ounce or 15 grams.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mebibit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2²⁰ (1048576) bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: sone
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of subjective sound loudness. One sone is the loudness of a pure tone of frequency one kilohertz and strength 40 decibels.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal second per steradian metre squared
pen gram (protein)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of grams of amino acid prescribed for parenteral/enteral therapy.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tex
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of yarn density. One decitex equals a mass of 1 gram per 1 kilometre of length.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megabit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁶ (1000000) bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: var
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: The name of the unit is an acronym for volt-ampere-reactive.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: watt per square metre kelvin to the fourth power
book
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of books (book: set of items bound together or written document of a material whole).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: round
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of rounds (round: A circular or cylindrical object).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of words
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of words.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megajoule per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of accumulated energy equal to one million joules per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ten day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of days in multiples of 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: day
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del tempo pari a 24 ore.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dry pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of pounds of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: decade
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of decades (decade: quantity equal to 10 or time equal to 10 years).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: standard kilolitre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the number of kilolitres of a product at a temperature of 15 degrees Celsius, especially in relation to hydrocarbon oils.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dozen piece
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pieces in multiples of 12 (piece: a single item, article or exemplar).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dozen pair
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pairs in multiples of 12 (pair: item described by two's).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: displacement tonnage
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the volume of sea water a ship displaces, expressed as the number of tons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dram (US)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: drachm (UK), troy dram

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dram (UK)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: avoirdupois dram

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dozen roll
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of rolls, expressed in twelve roll units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dry ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of tons of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: decitonne
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: centner, metric 100 kg; quintal, metric 100 kg

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dozen
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units in multiples of 12.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dozen pack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of packs in multiples of 12 (pack: standard packaging unit).
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Confezionamento standard con 12 pezzi.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A measure of pressure expressed in newtons per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megawatt hour per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of accumulated energy of a million watts over a period of one hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megawatt per hertz
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of energy expressed as the load change in million watts that will cause a frequency shift of one hertz.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milliampere hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power load delivered at the rate of one thousandth of an ampere over a period of one hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure used in meteorology and engineering to measure the demand for heating or cooling over a given period of days.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mille
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of cigarettes in units of 1000.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocalorie (international table)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of heat energy equal to one thousand calories.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocalorie (thermochemical) per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of energy equal to one thousand calories per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: million Btu(IT) per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power equal to one million British thermal units per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic foot per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one cubic foot passing a given point in a period of one second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tonne per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of weight or mass equal to one tonne per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ping
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of area equal to 3.3 square metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megabit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁶ (1000000) bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shares
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of shares (share: a total or portion of the parts into which a business entity’s capital is divided).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: TEU
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs) as a measure of containerized cargo capacity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tyre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of tyres (a solid or air-filled covering placed around a wheel rim to form a soft contact with the road, absorb shock and provide traction).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: active unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of active units within a substance.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dose
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of doses (dose: a definite quantity of a medicine or drug).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: air dry ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of tons of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: strand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of strands (strand: long, thin, flexible, single thread, strip of fibre, constituent filament or multiples of the same, twisted together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square metre per litre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of square metres per litre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: litre per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of litres per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: foot per thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of feet per thousand units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gigabyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁹ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: terabyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹² bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: petabyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹⁵ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pixel
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pixels (pixel: picture element).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megapixel
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count equal to 10⁶ (1000000) pixels (picture elements).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dots per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information defining the number of dots per linear inch as a measure of the resolution or sharpness of a graphic image.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gross kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the total number of kilograms before deductions.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: part per hundred thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 10⁻⁵.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram-force per square millimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of pressure defining the number of kilograms force per square millimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram-force per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of pressure defining the number of kilograms force per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of energy defining the number of joules per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram-force metre per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of torsion defining the torque kilogram-force metre per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt hour per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of energy consumption expressed as kilowatt hour per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt hour per kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of energy consumption expressed as kilowatt hour per kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: service unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of service units (service unit: defined period / property / facility / utility of supply).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: working day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of working days (working day: a day on which work is ordinarily performed).
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del conteggio che definisce il numero di giorni lavorativi (giorno lavorativo: un giorno in cui il lavoro viene normalmente eseguito).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: accounting unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of accounting units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: job
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of jobs.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: run foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number feet per run.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: test
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of tests.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: trip
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of trips.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: use
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of times an object is used.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: well
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of wells.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: zone
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of zones.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: exabit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹⁸ bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: exbibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁶⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pebibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁵⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tebibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁴⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gibibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2³⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mebibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2²⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kibibyte
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2¹⁰ bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: exbibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁶⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: exbibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁶⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: exbibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁶⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gigabyte per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10⁹ bytes per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gibibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2³⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gibibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2³⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gibibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2³⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kibibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2¹⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kibibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2¹⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kibibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2¹⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mebibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2²⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mebibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2²⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mebibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2²⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: petabit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹⁵ bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: petabit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹⁵ bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pebibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁵⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pebibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁵⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pebibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁵⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: terabit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹² bits (binary digits).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: terabit per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10¹² bits (binary digits) per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tebibit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁴⁰ bits (binary digits) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tebibit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁴⁰ bits (binary digits) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tebibit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 2⁴⁰ bits (binary digits) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bit per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 1 bit (binary digit) per metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bit per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 1 bit (binary digit) per square metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: each
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of items regarded as separate units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: electronic mail box
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of electronic mail boxes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: equivalent gallon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the number of gallons of product produced from concentrate.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bit per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 1 bit (binary digit) per cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: slug
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass. One slug is the mass accelerated at 1 foot per second per second by a force of 1 pound.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: rod [unit of distance]
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance equal to 5.5 yards (16 feet 6 inches).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milliampere per pound-force per square inch
water horse power
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power defining the amount of power required to move a given volume of water against acceleration of gravity to a specified elevation (pressure head).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO 80000-5 (Quantities and units — Part 5: Thermodynamics)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: fibre metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of length defining the number of metres of individual fibre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand cubic foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand cubic foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one hundred cubic metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: failures in time
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of failures that can be expected over a specified time interval. Failure rates of semiconductor components are often specified as FIT (failures in time unit) where 1 FIT = 10⁻⁹ /h.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: flake ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of tons of a flaked substance (flake: a small flattish fragment).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gigabecquerel
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce la miliardesima parte del becquerel.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram, dry weight
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of grams of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram of fissile isotope
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of grams of a fissile isotope (fissile isotope: an isotope whose nucleus is able to be split when irradiated with low energy neutrons).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: great gross
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units in multiples of 1728 (12 x 12 x 12).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram, including container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of grams of a product, including its container.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram, including inner packaging
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of grams of a product, including its inner packaging materials.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della massa, pari a un millesimo di un chilogrammo.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gross
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units in multiples of 144 (12 x 12).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square decametre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: are

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square hectometre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: hectare

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: blank
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of blanks.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to the SI base unit Kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton metre watt to the power minus 0,5
percent per month
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to a month.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per hectobar
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to 100-fold of the unit bar.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per decakelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to 10-fold of the SI base unit Kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: module width
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure used to describe the breadth of electronic assemblies as an installation standard or mounting dimension.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: French gauge
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance used for measuring the diameter of small tubes such as urological instruments and catheters. Synonym: French, Charrière, Charrière gauge

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: rack unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure used to describe the height in rack units of equipment intended for mounting in a 19-inch rack or a 23-inch rack. One rack unit is 1.75 inches (44.45 mm) high.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: big point
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of length defining the number of big points (big point: Adobe software(US) defines the big point to be exactly 1/72 inch (0.013 888 9 inch or 0.352 777 8 millimeters))

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: piece
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pieces (piece: a single item, article or exemplar).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per ohm
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to the SI derived unit ohm.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per degree
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to an angle of one degree.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per ten thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to multiples of ten thousand.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per one hundred thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to multiples of one hundred thousand.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per hundred
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to multiples of one hundred.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to multiples of one thousand.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per volt
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to the SI derived unit volt.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per bar
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to an atmospheric pressure of one bar.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to an inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to a metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hank
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of length, typically for yarn.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred boxes
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of boxes in multiples of one hundred box units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred count
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units counted in multiples of 100.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred kilogram, dry weight
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of hundred kilograms of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: head
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of heads (head: a person or animal considered as one of a number).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hectogram
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della massa pari a 100 g.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred cubic foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one hundred cubic foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred international unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of international units in multiples of 100.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred kilogram, net mass
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of hundred kilograms of a product, after deductions.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: million cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one million cubic metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hectolitre of pure alcohol
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one hundred litres of pure alcohol.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hour
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del tempo pari a 3 600 secondi o 60 minuti.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: person
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of persons.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: inch cubed

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: international sugar degree
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure defining the sugar content of a solution, expressed in degrees.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent per millimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to a millimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: per mille per psi
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of pressure equal to one thousandth of a psi (pound-force per square inch).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree API
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of relative density as a measure of how heavy or light a petroleum liquid is compared to water (API: American Petroleum Institute).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Baume (origin scale)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A traditional unit of relative density for liquids. Named after Antoine Baumé.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Baume (US heavy)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of relative density for liquids heavier than water.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Baume (US light)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of relative density for liquids lighter than water.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Balling
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of density as a measure of sugar content, especially of beer wort. Named after Karl Balling.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Brix
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion used in measuring the dissolved sugar-to-water mass ratio of a liquid. Named after Adolf Brix.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit hour square foot per British thermal unit (thermochemical)
degree Fahrenheit hour square foot per British thermal unit (international table)
degree Oechsle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of density as a measure of sugar content of must, the unfermented liqueur from which wine is made. Named after Ferdinand Oechsle.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Twaddell
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of density for liquids that are heavier than water. 1 degree Twaddle represents a difference in specific gravity of 0.005.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: baud
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of signal transmission speed equal to one signalling event per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) foot per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (international table) inch per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (international table) inch per second square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (international table) per pound degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (international table) per minute
British thermal unit (international table) per second
British thermal unit (thermochemical) foot per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (thermochemical) inch per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (thermochemical) inch per second square foot degree Fahrenheit
British thermal unit (thermochemical) per pound degree Fahrenheit
megabaud
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of signal transmission speed equal to 10⁶ (1000000) signaling events per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: calorie (international table) per gram degree Celsius
calorie (thermochemical) per centimetre second degree Celsius
pipeline joint
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A count of the number of pipeline joints.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hundred metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of 100 metre lengths.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of jewels
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of jewels (jewel: precious stone).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt demand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure defining the power load measured at predetermined intervals.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilovolt ampere reactive demand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure defining the reactive power demand equal to one kilovolt ampere of reactive power.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilovolt ampere reactive hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure defining the accumulated reactive energy equal to one kilovolt ampere of reactive power per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilobaud
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of signal transmission speed equal to 10³ (1000) signaling events per second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocalorie (international table) per hour metre degree Celsius
pound (avoirdupois) per cubic foot degree Fahrenheit
pound (avoirdupois) per cubic inch degree Fahrenheit
pound-force per square inch degree Fahrenheit
cake
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of cakes (cake: object shaped into a flat, compact mass).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: katal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of catalytic activity defining the catalytic activity of enzymes and other catalysts.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocharacter
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10³ (1000) characters.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of choline chloride
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of choline chloride.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram drained net weight
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the net number of kilograms of a product, disregarding the liquid content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO 80000-5 (Quantities and units — Part 5: Thermodynamics)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams.
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della massa pari a mille grammi.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of hydrogen peroxide
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of hydrogen peroxide.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram, including container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of kilograms of a product, including its container.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram, including inner packaging
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of kilograms of a product, including its inner packaging materials.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilosegment
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 10³ (1000) segments.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lactic dry material percentage
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion defining the percentage of dry lactic material in a product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilolux
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of illuminance equal to one thousand lux.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of methylamine
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of methylamine.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of weight expressed in kilograms of a substance that fills a volume of one cubic metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of nitrogen
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of nitrogen.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilonewton per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Pressure expressed in kN/m2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram named substance
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one kilogram of a named substance.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milliequivalence caustic potash per gram of product
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of milligrams of potassium hydroxide per gram of product as a measure of the concentration of potassium hydroxide in the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of potassium hydroxide (caustic potash)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of potassium hydroxide (caustic potash).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of potassium oxide
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of potassium oxide.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of phosphorus pentoxide (phosphoric anhydride)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of phosphorus pentoxide phosphoric anhydride.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of substance 90 % dry
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of a named substance that is 90% dry.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of sodium hydroxide (caustic soda)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of sodium hydroxide (caustic soda).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of kits (kit: tub, barrel or pail).
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Definisce il numero di kit di più articoli diversi tra di loro ma utilizzabili solo se insieme(ad esempio kit: viti e placche).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of uranium
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of uranium.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Kilowatt hour per normalized cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della potenza, pari a 1000 watt per ora.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of tungsten trioxide
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of tungsten trioxide.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Kilowatt hour per standard cubic metre
square metre hour degree Celsius per kilocalorie (international table)
ton short per hour degree Fahrenheit
lactose excess percentage
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion defining the percentage of lactose in a product that exceeds a defined percentage level.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: leaf
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of leaves.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: linear foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of feet (12-inch) in length of a uniform width object.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: labour hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of labour hours.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: link
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance equal to 0.01 chain.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: linear metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of metres in length of a uniform width object.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: length
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance defining the linear extent of an item measured from end to end.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lot [unit of procurement]
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of lots (lot: a collection of associated items).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: liquid pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of pounds of a liquid substance.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: litre of pure alcohol
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one litre of pure alcohol.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: layer
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of layers.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lump sum
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of whole or a complete monetary amounts.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ton (UK) or long ton (US)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: gross ton (2240 lb)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: litre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura della capacità nel sistema metrico decimale, pari al volume di 1 kg di acqua distillata alla temperatura di 4 °C, equivalente a 1,000028 dm³.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metric ton, lubricating oil
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of metric tons of lubricating oil.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: linear yard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of 36-inch units in length of a uniform width object.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Beaufort
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: An empirical measure for describing wind speed based mainly on observed sea conditions. The Beaufort scale indicates the wind speed by numbers that typically range from 0 for calm, to 12 for hurricane.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal megakelvin or megakelvin to the power minus one
percent per degree Celsius
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion, equal to 0.01, in relation to a temperature of one degree.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: 30-day month
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of months expressed in multiples of 30 days, one day equals 24 hours.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: actual/360
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of years expressed in multiples of 360 days, one day equals 24 hours.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilometre per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: centimetre per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,01-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: monetary value
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure expressed as a monetary amount.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: yard per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the length according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millimetre per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mile (statute mile) per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the length according to the Imperial system of units divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mil
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit to indicate an angle at military zone, equal to the 6400th part of the full circle of the 360° or 2·p·rad.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: revolution
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit to identify an angle of the full circle of 360° or 2·p·rad (Refer ISO/TC12 SI Guide).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree [unit of angle] per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 360 part of a full circle divided by the power of the SI base unit second and the exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: revolution per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the angular velocity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: circular mil
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of an area, of which the size is given by a diameter of length of 1 mm (0,001 in) based on the formula: area = p·(diameter/2)².

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square mile (based on U.S. survey foot)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the area, which is mainly common in the agriculture and forestry.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: chain (based on U.S. survey foot)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the length according the Anglo-American system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: furlong
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit commonly used in Great Britain at rural distances: 1 furlong = 40 rods = 10 chains (UK) = 1/8 mile = 1/10 furlong = 220 yards = 660 foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: foot (U.S. survey)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit commonly used in the United States for ordnance survey.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mile (based on U.S. survey foot)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit commonly used in the United States for ordnance survey.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit metre divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre per radiant
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the translation factor for implementation from rotation to linear movement.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shake
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for a very short period.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mile per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of velocity from the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mile per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the velocity from the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre per second pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit meter divided by the product of SI base unit second and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit metre divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch per year
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the length according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the unit common year with 365 days.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilometre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: yard per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit yard according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: yard per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit yard according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: yard per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit yard according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: acre-foot (based on U.S. survey foot)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the volume, which is used in the United States to measure/gauge the capacity of reservoirs.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cord (128 ft3)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Traditional unit of the volume of stacked firewood which has been measured with a cord.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic mile (UK statute)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of volume according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ton, register
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Traditional unit of the cargo capacity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic metre per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the SI base unit meter by exponent 3 divided by the derived SI base unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bel
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Logarithmic relationship to base 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per cubic metre pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit kilogram divided by the product of the power of the SI base unit metre with exponent 3 and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit kilogram divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilopound-force
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the unit of the force pound-force (lbf) according to the Anglo-American system of units with the relationship.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the power, which corresponds to a mass of a pound multiplied with the acceleration of a foot per square second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram metre per second squared
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the SI base unit kilogram and the SI base unit metre divided by the power of the SI base unit second by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pond
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the unit of the weight, defined as a mass of 1 kg which finds out about a weight strength from 1 kp by the gravitational force at sea level which corresponds to a strength of 9,806 65 newton.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square foot per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2 divided by the unit of time hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: stokes per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit stokes divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square centimetre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 1-fold of the power of the SI base unit metre by exponent 2 divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square metre per second pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the SI base unit metre with the exponent 2 divided by the SI base unit second and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: denier
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Traditional unit for the indication of the linear mass of textile fibers and yarns.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per yard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for linear mass according to avoirdupois system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ton, assay
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the mass used in the mineralogy to determine the concentration of precious metals in ore according to the mass of the precious metal in milligrams in a sample of the mass of an assay sound (number of troy ounces in a short ton (1 000 lb)).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pfund
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Outdated unit of the mass used in Germany.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per second pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit kilogram divided by the product of the SI base unit second and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tonne per month
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit tonne divided by the unit month.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tonne per year
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit tonne divided by the unit year with 365 days.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilopound per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the unit of the mass avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Proportion of the mass consisting of the avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system divided by the avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound-force foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the unit pound-force according to the Anglo-American system of units and the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton metre per radian
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the derived SI unit newton and the SI base unit metre divided by the unit radian.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of imbalance as a product of the SI base unit kilogram and the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the non SI-conforming unit of the force poundal and the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the non SI-conforming unit of the force poundal and the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dyne metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the rotational moment.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram centimetre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the SI base unit kilogram and the 0,01-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram centimetre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the 0,001-fold of the SI base unit kilogram and the 0,01-fold of the SI base unit metre divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megavolt ampere reactive hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electrical reactive power defining the total amount of reactive power across a power system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megavar
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electrical reactive power represented by a current of one thousand amperes flowing due a potential difference of one thousand volts where the sine of the phase angle between them is 1.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megawatt
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power defining the rate of energy transferred or consumed when a current of 1000 amperes flows due to a potential of 1000 volts at unity power factor.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand standard brick equivalent
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of one thousand brick equivalent units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand board foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand board foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microgram
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce la milionesima parte del grammo.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millicurie
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce 1/1000 l'attività di un radionuclide di un grammo dell'isotopo radio-226.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: air dry metric ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of metric tons of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milligram
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura di massa equivalente alla millesima parte del grammo.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: minute [unit of time]
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del tempo pari a 1/60 di un'ora.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: million international unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of international units in multiples of 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milliard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: billion (US)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millilitre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura che definisce la millesima parte del litro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic millimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità volumetrica, equivalente al volume di un cubo con lato di 1 millimetro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura di lunghezza equivalente alla millesima parte del metro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram, dry weight
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of kilograms of a product, disregarding the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Mega Joule per Normalised cubic Metre
month
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of time equal to 1/12 of a year of 365,25 days.
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del tempo pari a 1/12 di un anno di 365,25 giorni.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura per misurare le estensioni di un superficie.E' l''area racchiusa da un quadrato avente il lato lungo 1 metro.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: metre cubed
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura utilizzata per il gas in condizioni normali.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità base della lunghezza.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megawatt hour (1000 kW.h)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power defining the total amount of bulk energy transferred or consumed.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pen calorie
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of calories prescribed daily for parenteral/enteral therapy.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound foot per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system and the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound inch per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system and the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pferdestaerke
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Obsolete unit of the power relating to DIN 1301-3:1979: 1 PS = 735,498 75 W.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: centimetre of mercury (0 ºC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure, at which a value of 1 cmHg meets the static pressure, which is generated by a mercury at a temperature of 0 °C with a height of 1 centimetre .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: centimetre of water (4 ºC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure, at which a value of 1 cmH2O meets the static pressure, which is generated by a head of water at a temperature of 4 °C with a height of 1 centimetre .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: foot of water (39.2 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system for units, whereas the value of 1 ftH2O is equivalent to the static pressure, which is generated by a head of water at a temperature 39,2°F with a height of 1 foot .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch of mercury (32 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system for units, whereas the value of 1 inHg meets the static pressure, which is generated by a mercury at a temperature of 32°F with a height of 1 inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch of mercury (60 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system for units, whereas the value of 1 inHg meets the static pressure, which is generated by a mercury at a temperature of 60°F with a height of 1 inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch of water (39.2 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system for units, whereas the value of 1 inH2O meets the static pressure, which is generated by a head of water at a temperature of 39,2°F with a height of 1 inch .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch of water (60 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system for units, whereas the value of 1 inH2O meets the static pressure, which is generated by a head of water at a temperature of 60°F with a height of 1 inch .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kip per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the pressure according to the Anglo-American system of units as the 1000-fold of the unit of the force pound-force divided by the power of the unit inch by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of pressure by the Imperial system of units according to NIST: 1 pdl/ft² = 1,488 164 Pa.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ounce (avoirdupois) per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface specific mass (avoirdupois ounce according to the avoirdupois system of units according to the surface square inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: conventional metre of water
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Not SI-conforming unit of pressure, whereas a value of 1 mH2O is equivalent to the static pressure, which is produced by one metre high water column .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram per square millimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the SI base unit kilogram divided by the 0.000 001-fold of the power of the SI base unit meter by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per square yard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for areal-related mass as a unit pound according to the avoirdupois unit system divided by the power of the unit yard according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units with exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the pressure according to the Imperial system of units (poundal by square inch).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: foot to the fourth power
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 4 according to NIST: 1 ft4 = 8,630 975 m4.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic decimetre per kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001 fold of the power of the SI base unit meter by exponent 3 divided by the SI based unit kilogram.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic foot per pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 3 divided by the unit avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic inch per pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 3 divided by the avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois unit system .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilonewton per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit newton divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the surface tension according to the Imperial unit system as quotient poundal by inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound-force per yard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of force per unit length based on the Anglo-American system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal second per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of viscosity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poise per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit poise divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton second per square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a product of unit of the pressure (newton by square metre) multiplied with the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per metre second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a quotient SI base unit kilogram divided by the SI base unit metre and by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per metre minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a quotient SI base unit kilogram divided by the SI base unit metre and by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per metre day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a quotient SI base unit kilogram divided by the SI base unit metre and by the unit day.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per metre hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a quotient SI base unit kilogram divided by the SI base unit metre and by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gram per centimetre second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity as a quotient of the 0,001-fold of the SI base unit kilogram divided by the 0,01-fold of the SI base unit metre and SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: poundal second per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of dynamic viscosity according to the Imperial system of units as product unit of the pressure (poundal by square inch) multiplied by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per foot minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity according to the Anglo-American unit system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per foot day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the dynamic viscosity according to the Anglo-American unit system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic metre per second pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Power of the SI base unit meter by exponent 3 divided by the product of the SI base unit second and the derived SI base unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: foot poundal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the work (force-path).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: inch poundal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of work (force multiplied by path) according to the Imperial system of units as a product unit inch multiplied by poundal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: watt per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit watt divided by the power of the 0,01-fold the SI base unit metre by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: watt per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit watt divided by the power of the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per square foot hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per square foot hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per square foot minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per square foot second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per square foot second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per square inch second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: calorie (thermochemical) per square centimetre minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: calorie (thermochemical) per square centimetre second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the surface heat flux according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per cubic foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the energy density according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per cubic foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the energy density according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat capacity according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat capacity according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per degree Rankine
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat capacity according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per degree Rankine
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat capacity according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per pound degree Rankine
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat capacity (British thermal unit according to the international table according to the Rankine degree) according to the Imperial system of units divided by the unit avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocalorie (international table) per gram kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the mass-related heat capacity as quotient 1000-fold of the calorie (international table) divided by the product of the 0,001-fold of the SI base units kilogram and kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (39 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of heat energy according to the Imperial system of units in a reference temperature of 39 °F.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (59 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of heat energy according to the Imperial system of units in a reference temperature of 59 °F.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (60 ºF)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of head energy according to the Imperial system of units at a reference temperature of 60 °F.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: calorie (20 ºC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for quantity of heat, which is to be required for 1 g air free water at a constant pressure from 101,325 kPa, to warm up the pressure of standard atmosphere at sea level, from 19,5 °C on 20,5 °C.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: quad (1015 BtuIT)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of heat energy according to the imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: therm (EC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of heat energy in commercial use, within the EU defined: 1 thm (EC) = 100 000 BtuIT.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: therm (U.S.)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of heat energy in commercial use.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat energy according to the Imperial system of units divided the unit avoirdupois pound according to the avoirdupois system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat transition coefficient according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per hour square foot degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat transition coefficient according to the imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per second square foot degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat transition coefficient according to the imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per second square foot degree Fahrenheit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the heat transition coefficient according to the imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt per square metre kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit watt divided by the product of the power of the SI base unit metre by exponent 2 and the SI base unit kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kelvin per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit kelvin divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: watt per metre degree Celsius
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit watt divided by the product of the SI base unit metre and the unit for temperature degree Celsius.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt per metre kelvin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit watt divided by the product of the SI base unit metre and the SI base unit kelvin.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilowatt per metre degree Celsius
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit watt divided by the product of the SI base unit metre and the unit for temperature degree Celsius.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metre per degree Celcius metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit metre divided by the product of the unit degree Celsius and the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit hour per British thermal unit (international table)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit hour per British thermal unit (thermochemical)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit second per British thermal unit (international table)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit second per British thermal unit (thermochemical)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit hour square foot per British thermal unit (international table) inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of specific thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Fahrenheit hour square foot per British thermal unit (thermochemical) inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of specific thermal resistance according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilofarad
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit farad.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal joule
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Reciprocal of the derived SI unit joule.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: picosiemens
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit siemens.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ampere per pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit ampere divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: franklin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the electrical charge, where the charge amounts to exactly 1 Fr where the force of 1 dyn on an equal load is performed at a distance of 1 cm.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ampere minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of electric charge defining the amount of charge accumulated by a steady flow of one ampere for one minute..

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: biot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the electric power which is defined by a force of 2 dyn per cm between two parallel conductors of infinite length with negligible cross-section in the distance of 1 cm.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gilbert
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the magnetomotive force, which is defined by the work to increase the magnetic potential of a positive common pol with 1 erg.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: volt per pascal
