ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit volt divided by the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: picovolt
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit volt.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of articles
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of articles (article: item).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of cells
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of cells (cell: an enclosed or circumscribed space, cavity, or volume).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: message
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of messages.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nil
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of instances of nothing.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of international units
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of international units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: load
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the number of loads (load: a quantity of items carried or processed at one time).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Normalised cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Normalised cubic metre (temperature 0°C and pressure 101325 millibars )

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of packs
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of packs (pack: a collection of objects packaged together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: number of parts
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of parts (part: component of a larger entity).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: net ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to 2000 pounds, see ton (US). Refer International Convention on tonnage measurement of Ships.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: part per thousand
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 10⁻³. Synonym: per mille

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: panel
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of panels (panel: a distinct, usually rectangular, section of a surface).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ozone depletion equivalent
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the ozone depletion potential in kilograms of a product relative to the calculated depletion for the reference substance, Trichlorofluoromethane (CFC-11).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: oscillations per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: The number of oscillations per minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: overtime hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of overtime hours.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion equal to 0.01.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: coulomb per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit coulomb divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kiloweber
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000 fold of the derived SI unit weber.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gamma
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of magnetic flow density.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilotesla
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit tesla.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient of the derived SI unit joule divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit day.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilojoule per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the 1000-fold of the derived SI unit joule divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilojoule per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the 1000-fold of the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilojoule per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the 1000-fold of the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilojoule per day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Quotient from the 1000-fold of the derived SI unit joule divided by the unit day.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanoohm
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit ohm.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ohm circular-mil per foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of resistivity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilohenry
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit henry.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lumen per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit lumen divided by the power of the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: phot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of luminance, defined as lumen by square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: footcandle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI conform traditional unit, defined as density of light which impinges on a surface which has a distance of one foot from a light source, which shines with an intensity of an international candle.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: candela per square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit candela divided by the power of unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: footlambert
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the luminance according to the Anglo-American system of units, defined as emitted or reflected luminance of a lm/ft².

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: lambert
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of luminance, defined as the emitted or reflected luminance by one lumen per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: stilb
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of luminance, defined as emitted or reflected luminance by one lumen per square centimetre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: candela per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Base unit SI candela divided by the power of the unit foot according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilocandela
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the SI base unit candela.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millicandela
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the SI base unit candela.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Hefner-Kerze
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Obsolete, non-legal unit of the power in Germany relating to DIN 1301-3:1979: 1 HK = 0,903 cd.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: international candle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Obsolete, non-legal unit of the power in Germany relating to DIN 1301-3:1979: 1 HK = 1,019 cd.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (international table) per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the areal-related energy transmission according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: British thermal unit (thermochemical) per square foot
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the areal-related energy transmission according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: calorie (thermochemical) per square centimetre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the areal-related energy transmission according to the Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: langley
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the areal-related energy transmission (as a measure of the incident quantity of heat of solar radiation on the earth's surface).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: decade (logarithmic)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1 Dec := log2 10 ˜ 3,32 according to the logarithm for frequency range between f1 and f2, when f2/f1 = 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pascal squared second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the set as a product of the power of derived SI unit pascal with exponent 2 and the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: bel per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit bel divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound mole
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of quantity of a substance relating that one pound mole of a chemical composition corresponds to the same number of pounds as the molecular weight of one molecule of this composition in atomic mass units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound mole per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the power of the amount of substance non-SI compliant unit of the molar flux relating that a pound mole of a chemical composition the same number of pound corresponds like the molecular weight of a molecule of this composition in atomic mass units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound mole per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the power of the amount of substance non-SI compliant unit of the molar flux relating that a pound mole of a chemical composition the same number of pound corresponds like the molecular weight of a molecule of this composition in atomic mass units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilomole per kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the SI base unit mol divided by the SI base unit kilogram.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound mole per pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of the material molar flux divided by the avoirdupois pound for mass according to the avoirdupois unit system.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton square metre per ampere
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the derived SI unit newton and the power of SI base unit metre with exponent 2 divided by the SI base unit ampere.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: five pack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of five-packs (five-pack: set of five items packaged together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: weber metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Product of the derived SI unit weber and SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mol per kilogram pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit mol divided by the product of the SI base unit kilogram and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mol per cubic metre pascal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI base unit mol divided by the product of the power from the SI base unit metre with exponent 3 and the derived SI unit pascal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: unit pole
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit for magnetic flux of a magnetic pole (according to the interaction of identical poles of 1 dyn at a distance of a cm).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milligray per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microgray per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanogray per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gray per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI derived unit gray divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milligray per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microgray per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanogray per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: gray per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: SI derived unit gray divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: milligray per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microgray per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanogray per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit gray divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: sievert per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit sievert divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millisievert per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microsievert per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanosievert per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: rem per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for the equivalent tin rate relating to DIN 1301-3:1979: 1 rem/s = 0,01 J/(kg·s) = 1 Sv/s.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: sievert per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millisievert per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microsievert per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanosievert per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: sievert per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millisievert per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microsievert per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: nanosievert per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,000 000 001-fold of the derived SI unit sievert divided by the unit minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal square inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Complement of the power of the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pascal square metre per kilogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the burst index as derived unit for pressure pascal related to the substance, represented as a quotient from the SI base unit kilogram divided by the power of the SI base unit metre by exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: millipascal per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 0,001-fold of the derived SI unit pascal divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilopascal per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the derived SI unit pascal divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hectopascal per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 100-fold of the derived SI unit pascal divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: standard atmosphere per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Outdated unit of the pressure divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: technical atmosphere per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Obsolete and non-legal unit of the pressure which is generated by a 10 metre water column divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: torr per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: CGS (Centimetre-Gram-Second system) unit of the pressure divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: psi per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Compound unit for pressure (pound-force according to the Anglo-American unit system divided by the power of the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units with the exponent 2) divided by the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cubic metre per second square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of volume flow cubic meters by second related to the transmission surface in square metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: rhe
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Non SI-conforming unit of fluidity of dynamic viscosity.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound-force foot per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for length-related rotational moment according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound-force inch per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for length-related rotational moment according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: perm (0 ºC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Traditional unit for the ability of a material to allow the transition of the steam, defined at a temperature of 0 °C as steam transmittance, where the mass of one grain steam penetrates an area of one foot squared at a pressure from one inch mercury per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: perm (23 ºC)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Traditional unit for the ability of a material to allow the transition of the steam, defined at a temperature of 23 °C as steam transmittance at which the mass of one grain of steam penetrates an area of one square foot at a pressure of one inch mercury per hour.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: byte per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit byte divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilobyte per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1000-fold of the unit byte divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megabyte per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: 1 000 000-fold of the unit byte divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal volt
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Reciprocal of the derived SI unit volt.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal radian
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Reciprocal of the unit radian.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pascal to the power sum of stoichiometric numbers
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the equilibrium constant on the basis of the pressure(ISO 80000-9:2009, 9-35.a).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mole per cubiv metre to the power sum of stoichiometric numbers
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the equilibrium constant on the basis of the concentration (ISO 80000-9:2009, 9-36.a).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pad
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pads (pad: block of paper sheets fastened together at one end).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: proof litre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one litre of proof spirits, or the alcohol equivalent thereof. Used for measuring the strength of distilled alcoholic liquors, expressed as a percentage of the alcohol content of a standard mixture at a specific temperature.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: proof gallon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one gallon of proof spirits, or the alcohol equivalent thereof. Used for measuring the strength of distilled alcoholic liquors, expressed as a percentage of the alcohol content of a standard mixture at a specific temperature.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pitch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of characters that fit in a horizontal inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: degree Plato
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of proportion defining the sugar content of a product, especially in relation to beer.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: page per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of quantity defining the degree of thickness of a bound publication, expressed as the number of pages per inch of thickness.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pair
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pairs (pair: item described by two's).
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di misura del conteggio che definisce il numero di coppie (ad esempio coppia: oggetto descritto da due).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: portion
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A quantity of allowance of food allotted to, or enough for, one person.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: joule per tesla
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the magnetic dipole moment of the molecule as derived SI unit joule divided by the derived SI unit tesla.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: erlang
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the market value according to the feature of a single feature as a statistical measurement of the existing utilization.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: octet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym for byte: 1 octet = 8 bit = 1 byte.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: octet per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit octet divided by the SI base unit second.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shannon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of two mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hartley
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of ten mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: natural unit of information
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of ,718 281 828 459 mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base Euler value e.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shannon per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Time related logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of two mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hartley per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Time related logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of ten mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: natural unit of information per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Time related logarithmic unit for information equal to the content of decision of a sentence of 2,718 281 828 459 mutually exclusive events, expressed as a logarithm to base of the Euler value e.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: second per kilogramm
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the Einstein transition probability for spontaneous or inducing emissions and absorption according to ISO 80000-7:2008, expressed as SI base unit second divided by the SI base unit kilogram.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: watt square metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the first radiation constants c1 = 2·p·h·c0², the value of which is 3,741 771 18·10?¹6-fold that of the comparative value of the product of the derived SI unit watt multiplied with the power of the SI base unit metre with the exponent 2.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: second per radian cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit of the density of states as an expression of angular frequency as complement of the product of hertz and radiant and the power of SI base unit metre by exponent 3 .

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: weber to the power minus one
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Complement of the derived SI unit weber as unit of the Josephson constant, which value is equal to the 384 597,891-fold of the reference value gigahertz divided by volt.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: reciprocal inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Complement of the unit inch according to the Anglo-American and Imperial system of units.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: dioptre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit used at the statement of relative refractive indexes of optical systems as complement of the focal length with correspondence to: 1 dpt = 1/m.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: one per one
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Value of the quotient from two physical units of the same kind as a numerator and denominator whereas the units are shortened mutually.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: newton metre per metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for length-related rotational moment as product of the derived SI unit newton and the SI base unit metre divided by the SI base unit metre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram per square metre pascal second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Unit for the ability of a material to allow the transition of steam.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: microgram per hectogram
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Microgram per hectogram.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pH (potential of Hydrogen)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: The activity of the (solvated) hydrogen ion (a logarithmic measure used to state the acidity or alkalinity of a chemical solution).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: megawatts per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of power defining the total amount of bulk energy transferred or consumer per minute.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square metre per cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of the amount of surface area per unit volume of an object or collection of objects.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Standard cubic metre per day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard cubic metre (temperature 15°C and pressure 101325 millibars ) per day

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Standard cubic metre per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard cubic metre (temperature 15°C and pressure 101325 millibars ) per hour

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Normalized cubic metre per day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Normalized cubic metre (temperature 0°C and pressure 101325 millibars ) per day

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Normalized cubic metre per hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Normalized cubic metre (temperature 0°C and pressure 101325 millibars ) per hour

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: meal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of meals (meal: an amount of food to be eaten on a single occasion).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: page - facsimile
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of facsimile pages.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: quarter (of a year)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of quarters (3 months).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: page - hardcopy
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of hardcopy pages (hardcopy page: a page rendered as printed or written output on paper, film, or other permanent medium).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: quire
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count for paper, expressed as the number of quires (quire: a number of paper sheets, typically 25).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: quarter (UK)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A traditional unit of weight equal to 1/4 hundredweight. In the United Kingdom, one quarter equals 28 pounds.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pica
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of picas. (pica: typographical length equal to 12 points or 4.22 mm (approx.)).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand cubic metres.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: running or operating hour
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of hours of operation.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ream
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count for paper, expressed as the number of reams (ream: a large quantity of paper sheets, typically 500).
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Unità di conteggio per la carta, espressa come numero di risme.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: room
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of rooms.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: pound per ream
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass for paper, expressed as pounds per ream. (ream: a large quantity of paper, typically 500 sheets).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: revolutions per minute
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO/TC12 SI Guide

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: revolutions per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Refer ISO/TC12 SI Guide

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: revenue ton mile
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information typically used for billing purposes, expressed as the number of revenue tons (revenue ton: either a metric ton or a cubic metres, whichever is the larger), moved over a distance of one mile.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square foot per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: foot squared per second

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square metre per second
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: metre squared per second (square metres/second US)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: half year (6 months)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of half years (6 months).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: score
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of units in multiples of 20.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: set
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of sets (set: a number of objects grouped together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: segment
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information equal to 64000 bytes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Standard cubic metre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard cubic metre (temperature 15°C and pressure 101325 millibars )

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of squares (square: rectangular shape).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: square, roofing
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of squares of roofing materials, measured in multiples of 100 square feet.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: strip
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of strips (strip: long narrow piece of an object).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: stick
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of sticks (stick: slender and often cylindrical piece of a substance).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: stick, cigarette
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of cigarettes in the smallest unit for stock-taking and/or duty computation.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: standard litre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume defining the number of litres of a product at a temperature of 15 degrees Celsius, especially in relation to hydrocarbon oils.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ton (US) or short ton (UK/US)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: net ton (2000 lb)

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: straw
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of straws (straw: a slender tube used for sucking up liquids).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: skein
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of skeins (skein: a loosely-coiled bundle of yarn or thread).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: shipment
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of shipments (shipment: an amount of goods shipped or transported).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: syringe
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of syringes (syringe: a small device for pumping, spraying and/or injecting liquids through a small aperture).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: telecommunication line in service
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of lines in service.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand piece
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pieces in multiples of 1000 (piece: a single item, article or exemplar).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: total acid number
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of chemistry defining the amount of potassium hydroxide (KOH) in milligrams that is needed to neutralize the acids in one gram of oil. It is an important quality measurement of crude oil.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metric ton, including container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of metric tons of a product, including its container.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: metric ton, including inner packaging
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of metric tons of a product, including its inner packaging materials.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tonne kilometre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of information typically used for billing purposes, expressed as the number of tonnes (metric tons) moved over a distance of one kilometre.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: kilogram of imported meat, less offal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass equal to one thousand grams of imported meat, disregarding less valuable by-products such as the entrails.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tonne (metric ton)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Synonym: metric ton

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ten pack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of items in multiples of 10.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: teeth per inch
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: The number of teeth per inch.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ten pair
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pairs in multiples of 10 (pair: item described by two's).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: thousand cubic metre per day
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand cubic metres per day.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ten set
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of sets in multiples of 10 (set: a number of objects grouped together).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: ten thousand sticks
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of sticks in multiples of 10000 (stick: slender and often cylindrical piece of a substance).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: treatment
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of treatments (treatment: subjection to the action of a chemical, physical or biological agent).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: tablet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of tablets (tablet: a small flat or compressed solid object).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: telecommunication line in service average
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the average number of lines in service.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: telecommunication port
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of network access ports.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: percent volume
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A measure of concentration, typically expressed as the percentage volume of a solute in a solution.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: wet kilo
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of kilograms of a product, including the water content of the product.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: wet pound
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of pounds of a material, including the water content of the material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: cord
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume used for measuring lumber. One board foot equals 1/12 of a cubic foot.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: wet ton
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of mass defining the number of tons of a material, including the water content of the material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: wine gallon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume equal to 231 cubic inches.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: working month
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of time defining the number of working months.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: standard
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of volume of finished lumber equal to 165 cubic feet. Synonym: standard cubic foot

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Gunter's chain
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of distance used or formerly used by British surveyors.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: hanging container
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of hanging containers.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: page
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of count defining the number of pages.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: mutually defined
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A unit of measure as agreed in common between two or more parties.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Container, flexible
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A packaging container of flexible construction.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag, super bulk
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A cloth plastic or paper based bag having the dimensions of the pallet on which it is constructed.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag, polybag
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of plastic bag, typically used to wrap promotional pieces, publications, product samples, and/or catalogues.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Case, car
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of portable container designed to store equipment for carriage in an automobile.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Case, wooden
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A case made of wood for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, wooden
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A platform or open-ended box, made of wood, on which goods are retained for ease of mechanical handling during transport and storage.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Crate, wooden
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A receptacle, made of wood, on which goods are retained for ease of mechanical handling during transport and storage.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bundle, wooden
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Loose or unpacked pieces of wood tied or wrapped together.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, fibre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of fibre used for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, paper
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of paper for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, wooden
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of wood for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, modular, collars 80cms * 60cms
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard sized pallet of dimensions 80 centimeters by 60 centimeters (cms).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, shrinkwrapped
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Pallet load secured with transparent plastic film that has been wrapped around and then shrunk tightly.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, 100cms * 110cms
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard sized pallet of dimensions 100centimeters by 110 centimeters (cms).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Cone
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Container used in the transport of linear material such as yarn.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Ball
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A spherical containment vessel for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Belt
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A band use to retain multiple articles together.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A receptacle made of flexible material with an open or closed top.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bottle, non-protected, cylindrical
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A narrow-necked cylindrical shaped vessel without external protective packing material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bottle, protected cylindrical
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A narrow-necked cylindrical shaped vessel with external protective packing material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bottle, non-protected, bulbous
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A narrow-necked bulb shaped vessel without external protective packing material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bottle, protected bulbous
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A narrow-necked bulb shaped vessel with external protective packing material.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Card
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A flat package usually made of fibreboard from/to which product is often hung or attached.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Container, not otherwise specified as transport equipment
Cartridge
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Package containing a charge such as propelling explosive for firearms or ink toner for a printer.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Box, Commonwealth Handling Equipment Pool (CHEP), Eurobox
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A box mounted on a pallet base under the control of CHEP.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tray, one layer no cover, plastic
Tray, one layer no cover, wooden
Tray, one layer no cover, polystyrene
Tray, one layer no cover, cardboard

Tray, two layers no cover, wooden
Tray, two layers no cover, cardboard
Flexibag
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A flexible containment bag made of plastic, typically for the transportation bulk non-hazardous cargoes using standard size shipping containers.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Flexitank
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A flexible containment tank made of plastic, typically for the transportation bulk non-hazardous cargoes using standard size shipping containers.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Cart, flatbed
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Wheeled flat bedded device on which trays or other regular shaped items are packed for transportation purposes.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bottle, gas
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A narrow-necked metal cylinder for retention of liquefied or compressed gas.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Container, gallon
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A container with a capacity of one gallon.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, glass
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of glass for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tray, containing horizontally stacked flat items
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Tray containing flat items stacked on top of one another.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag, gunny
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A sack made of gunny or burlap, used for transporting coarse commodities, such as grains, potatoes, and other agricultural products.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Hanger
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A purpose shaped device with a hook at the top for hanging items from a rail.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Package, flow
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A flexible tubular package or skin, possibly transparent, often used for containment of foodstuffs (e.g. salami sausage).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Package, cardboard, with bottle grip-holes
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Packaging material made out of cardboard that facilitates the separation of individual glass or plastic bottles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tray, rigid, lidded stackable (CEN TS 14482:2002)
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Lidded stackable rigid tray compliant with CEN TS 14482:2002.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag, jumbo
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A flexible containment bag, widely used for storage, transportation and handling of powder, flake or granular materials. Typically constructed from woven polypropylene (PP) fabric in the form of cubic bags.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Kit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A set of articles or implements used for a specific purpose.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Luggage
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A collection of bags, cases and/or containers which hold personal belongings for a journey.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Lug
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A wooden box for the transportation and storage of fruit or vegetables.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Liftvan
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A wooden or metal container used for packing household goods and personal effects.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Crate, metal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment box made of metal for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Container, metal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of containment box made of metal for retaining substances or articles, not otherwise specified as transport equipment.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, metal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of metal for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, plastic wrapped
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel wrapped with plastic for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Two sided cage on wheels with fixing strap

Plastic pallet SRS 80 cm x 120 cm

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Commonwealth Handling Equipment Pool (CHEP) standard pallet of dimensions 100 centimeters x 120 centimeters.

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Australian standard pallet of dimensions 115.5 centimeters x 116.5 centimeters.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, ISO T11
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: ISO standard pallet of dimensions 110 centimeters x 110 centimeters, prevalent in Asia - Pacific region.

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A pallet equivalent shipping platform of unknown dimensions or unknown weight.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Block
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A solid piece of a hard substance, such as granite, having one or more flat sides.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Octabin
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A standard cardboard container of large dimensions for storing for example vegetables, granules of plastics or other dry products.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Container, outer
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of containment box that serves as the outer shipping container, not otherwise specified as transport equipment.

A Wheeled pallet with raised rim (81 x 72 x 135)

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A shallow, wide, open container, usually of metal.

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Small package.

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard sized pallet of dimensions 80 centimeters by 100 centimeters (cms).

ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard sized pallet of dimensions 80 centimeters by 120 centimeters (cms).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pen
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A small open top enclosure for retaining animals.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Package
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Standard packaging unit.
ns0:Code.ns0:LocalDescription: Definisce il pacchetto che contiene più unità singole e' il confezionamento minimo vendibile (Es. confezione di compresse).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Piece
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A loose or unpacked article.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Receptacle, plastic
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment vessel made of plastic for retaining substances or articles.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Platform or open-ended box, usually made of wood, on which goods are retained for ease of mechanical handling during transport and storage.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Box, wooden, natural wood, with sift proof walls
Reel
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Cylindrical rotatory device with a rim at each end on which materials are wound.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Rednet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Containment material made of red mesh netting for retaining articles (e.g. trees).

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Skid
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A low movable platform or pallet to facilitate the handling and transport of goods.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Slipsheet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Hard plastic sheeting primarily used as the base on which to stack goods to optimise the space within a container. May be used as an alternative to a palletized packaging.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Spool
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A packaging container used in the transport of such items as wire, cable, tape and yarn.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Shrinkwrapped
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Goods retained in a transparent plastic film that has been wrapped around and then shrunk tightly on to the goods.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tablet
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A loose or unpacked article in the form of a bar, block or piece.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tyre
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A ring made of rubber and/or metal surrounding a wheel.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tank container, generic
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A specially constructed container for transporting liquids and gases in bulk.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bag, tote
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A capacious bag or basket.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Tube, with nozzle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A tube made of plastic, metal or cardboard fitted with a nozzle, containing a liquid or semi-liquid product, e.g. silicon.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Pallet, triwall
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A lightweight pallet made from heavy duty corrugated board.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Unit
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of package composed of a single item or object, not otherwise specified as a unit of transport equipment.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Vanpack
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A type of wooden crate.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Vehicle
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: A self-propelled means of conveyance.

ns0:Code.ns0:Name: Bulk, scrap metal
ns0:Code.ns0:Description: Loose or unpacked scrap metal transported in bulk form.
